title: Does Water Have Memory? Science Says No
script_summary: This video debunks the myth of water memory using chemistry.

segments.voiceover: Can water really remember? Science says no.
In homeopathy, some claim water holds memory of substances.
But chemistry tells a different story.
Hydrogen bonds in water are fleeting, lasting only microseconds.
And when diluted beyond Avogadro's number?
There’s virtually no solute left. Just pure water.
So, water’s memory? It’s a myth, not science.
Like and follow for more science debunking!

full_voiceover: Can water really remember? Science says no. In homeopathy, some claim water holds memory of substances. But chemistry tells a different story. Hydrogen bonds in water are fleeting, lasting only microseconds. And when diluted beyond Avogadro's number? There’s virtually no solute left. Just pure water. So, water’s memory? It’s a myth, not science. Like and follow for more science debunking!
